div1.entryFree: Idea, image: the i. of her life shall sweetly creep into his study of imagination, Ado IV, 1, 226. a foolish extravagant spirit, full of forms, figures, shapes, objects, --s, apprehensions, motions, revolutions, LLL IV, 2, 69. I did infer your lineaments, being the right i. of your father, both in your form and nobleness of mind, R3 III, 7, 13.
